hypotheses.hypothesis: Education-First: University educators teaching game theory, strategic management, or negotiation are the beachhead segment for an integrated platform combining evidence retrieval, game-theory analysis, classroom management, and forecast governance.
load_bearing_claims: Educators need an integrated platform (not just experiments or computation)
Game theory + evidence + forecasting is more valuable than game theory alone
ClassroomManager and academic features are production-ready
$34/mo academic pricing is acceptable to educators/departments
Educators will switch from MobLab/Wolfram/manual methods
Evidence retrieval and citations add value for educational use
Game theory courses are growing and need AI-resistant teaching tools
Educators are reachable through .edu verification, conferences, and direct outreach
Forecast governance is valuable in education
Game-theory learning platform is a recognizable category for educators
hypotheses.evidence_support: Codebase evidence shows ClassroomManager, education modes, .edu verification. Market research shows MobLab/Wolfram gaps. No customer evidence.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Game theory adoption concerns in education. AI chatbot substitution threat. No customer evidence. MobLab is entrenched with 60+ games, gradebook, Canvas/Blackboard sync.

evidence_bundle.claim: Educators need an integrated platform (not just experiments or computation)
ClassroomManager and academic features are production-ready
Game theory courses are growing and need AI-resistant teaching tools

hypotheses.hypothesis: Enterprise-First (Current Positioning): Enterprise strategy, geopolitical risk, and public-sector foresight teams are the primary segment for governed strategic intelligence.
load_bearing_claims: Enterprise teams need governed game-theoretic analysis
SIP can compete with Recorded Future, Palantir, S&P Global
$199/mo enterprise pricing fits enterprise budgets
hypotheses.evidence_support: Product positioning docs target enterprise. Codebase has enterprise features.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Low reachability (3/10). High competition (8/10). Low product fit (5/10). No proof (2/10). Pricing mismatch ($199/mo vs $50K-$150K+ enterprise budgets). Game theory not an enterprise buying trigger. 0 customers.

evidence_bundle.claim: Enterprise teams need governed game-theoretic analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Consultant Analysis Tool: Independent strategy consultants need structured, evidence-backed analysis tools for client engagements.
load_bearing_claims: Consultants need structured analysis tools (not just spreadsheets)
Evidence retrieval + game theory is more valuable than AI chatbot alone
$49-$199/mo is acceptable for consultants who can expense tools
PDF export meets consulting deliverable standards
Consultants will adopt a new tool for client work
hypotheses.evidence_support: Codebase has PDF export, analysis features. Market research shows Portage, CXO Scenario Planner.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: AI chatbot substitution threat. High competition from emerging tools. No customer evidence.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Negotiation Training Platform: Corporate negotiation trainers need interactive, AI-powered practice tools with game-theory-based scenarios and cognitive bias detection.
load_bearing_claims: Trainers need AI-powered practice tools (not just role-play)
Bias detection and strategic DNA add value to negotiation training
$49-$199/mo is acceptable for training companies
NegotiationDojo is production-ready for training use
Trainers will adopt a new tool alongside existing methods
hypotheses.counter_evidence: No direct competitor validation. Market size unclear. No customer evidence.

evidence_bundle.claim: NegotiationDojo is production-ready for training use

hypotheses.hypothesis: Evidence-Backed Assignment Platform: University professors need evidence-backed analysis assignments where students retrieve evidence, model strategic interactions, and produce governed forecasts.
load_bearing_claims: Professors need evidence-backed analysis assignments, not just experiments
Evidence retrieval + citations differentiate from MobLab's experiment-only approach
Forecast governance is teachable and valuable in education
Per-student pricing model (like MobLab $12-$25) is viable
LTI integration enables Canvas/Moodle adoption
AI-resistant assessment tools are in demand due to ChatGPT
hypotheses.evidence_support: Codebase has LTI launch, evidence retrieval, classroom assignments. MobLab lacks evidence/governance (EV-045).
hypotheses.counter_evidence: MobLab is entrenched with 60+ games, gradebook, Canvas/Blackboard sync. SIP lacks LMS grade passback. No per-student pricing model. LTI JWKS uses placeholder key.

evidence_bundle.claim: Evidence retrieval + citations differentiate from MobLab
AI-resistant assessment tools are in demand due to ChatGPT

hypotheses.hypothesis: Policy Think Tank Analysis Tool: Policy researchers at think tanks need game-theoretic modeling with evidence retrieval and GDELT integration for transparent, reproducible policy analysis.
load_bearing_claims: Think tank analysts need game-theoretic modeling tools
Evidence retrieval + GDELT integration provides unique value
Forecast governance enables reproducible policy analysis
Think tanks have budget for analysis tools
Think tanks are reachable through policy networks
hypotheses.evidence_support: GDELT integration exists in code. Evidence retrieval implemented.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: GDELT requires GCP setup (not configured). No think tank customer evidence. Think tanks may use proprietary models.

evidence_bundle.claim: Evidence retrieval + GDELT integration provides unique value

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI-Resistant Assessment Tool: University professors need AI-resistant assessment tools that require students to gather evidence, model strategic interactions, and produce governed forecasts with provenance trails.
load_bearing_claims: AI substitution is a top concern for professors (2025-2026)
Evidence-gathering + game-theory modeling workflow is AI-resistant
Professors will adopt new assessment tools to combat AI cheating
Provenance trails and governance make AI substitution detectable
This is a recognizable category professors will search for
hypotheses.evidence_support: Claim registry and forecast governance implemented in code. AI compromising assessments evidence (EV-038).
hypotheses.counter_evidence: AI detection tools already exist (Turnitin). Professors may prefer exams over new software. No evidence professors are searching for 'AI-resistant assessment'.

evidence_bundle.claim: AI substitution is a top concern for professors
Provenance trails and governance make AI substitution detectable

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strategic Thinking Prosumer Platform: Strategy professionals, independent analysts, and lifelong learners will pay for structured strategic analysis tools with game-theory reasoning and bias assessment.
load_bearing_claims: Prosumers will pay $19-$49/mo for strategic analysis tools
Structured workflow is more valuable than ChatGPT for serious analysts
NegotiationDojo and bias profile are compelling prosumer features
Prosumers are reachable through content marketing and communities
ChatGPT is not a sufficient substitute for structured analysis
hypotheses.evidence_support: Pricing tiers exist in config. NegotiationDojo and BiasProfile implemented.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: ChatGPT is free and improving rapidly. Prosumer market for game theory is unproven. No evidence of WTP.

evidence_bundle.claim: Prosumers will pay $19-$49/mo for strategic analysis tools

hypotheses.hypothesis: MobLab Complement (Not Replacement): Professors already using MobLab for classroom experiments want a complementary tool for evidence-backed real-world scenario analysis and forecast governance.
load_bearing_claims: Professors using MobLab want complementary analysis tools
SIP can integrate with MobLab workflows (not compete)
Evidence + governance fills MobLab's gap
Professors will adopt a second tool alongside MobLab
Per-student pricing for SIP complement is viable alongside MobLab's $12-$25
hypotheses.evidence_support: MobLab lacks evidence/governance (EV-045). SIP has evidence retrieval and forecast governance.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Professors may not want two tools. MobLab could add evidence features. No evidence of complementary demand.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Governance-First Platform: Enterprise strategy and compliance teams need AI-assisted decisions with mandatory human review, evidence-bounded reasoning, and audit trails meeting EU AI Act and SOC 2 requirements.
load_bearing_claims: EU AI Act creates budget for governance tools
Game theory + governance is more valuable than governance alone
SIP's human review workflow meets compliance requirements
Enterprise teams will adopt SIP over specialized governance platforms
$199/mo enterprise pricing fits governance tool budgets
hypotheses.evidence_support: Governance platforms identified (DecisionLedger EV-068, Qonera EV-070, Coalex EV-072, KiteEdge EV-069). EU AI Act compliance window.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: SIP lacks SOC 2 / ISO 42001 certifications. SIP lacks enterprise connectors (DecisionLedger has 150+). EU AI Act Article 50 compliance August 2026. SIP has 0 enterprise sales capability.

evidence_bundle.claim: EU AI Act creates budget for governance tools
